text.headline: The Geographic Arc
text.text: A climate scientist's journey from Austin to Stanford, Princeton, Carnegie, and back home to UT Austin.

text.headline: Austin / Childhood
text.text: Geeta Persad grew up in Austin, exploring the Texas Memorial Museum and attending UT Austin lectures as a child.

text.headline: Stanford / Undergraduate
text.text: B.S. in Geophysics from Stanford University (2010) — the first step toward a career in climate science.

text.headline: Princeton / Doctoral Formation
text.text: Ph.D. in Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences (2016), with NSF fellowship and STEP policy certificate. Dissertation on atmospheric aerosols and climate impacts.

text.headline: Carnegie & UCS / Postdoctoral Bridge
text.text: Joint position at Carnegie Institution for Science and Union of Concerned Scientists, bridging fundamental science and policy application.

text.headline: UT Austin / Faculty
text.text: Assistant Professor at Jackson School of Geosciences (2020–present) — returning home to lead the Persad Aero-Climate Group.
